changes.value: Add native CoreML wrapper to onnxruntime-coreml-sys

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1 +1 @@
-Add Objective-C wrapper functions for direct CoreML .mlpackage loading, alongside the existing ONNX Runtime wrappers. This lets ane-embedding load CoreML models natively without going through ORT's conversion.

The existing crate already links CoreML, Metal, Foundation frameworks (build.rs). We just need new wrapper functions.

**New files:**
- `onnxruntime-coreml-sys/src/coreml_wrapper.m` — Objective-C implementation
- `onnxruntime-coreml-sys/src/coreml_wrapper.h` — C header

**API surface (C functions callable from Rust):**
```c
// Load a .mlpackage from disk
int coreml_load_model(const char* path, CoreMLModel** out, CoreMLError* err);

// Get input/output metadata
int coreml_model_input_names(CoreMLModel* model, ...);
int coreml_model_output_names(CoreMLModel* model, ...);

// Create input feature provider from tensors
int coreml_create_input(const char* name, const float* data, 
                        const int64_t* shape, int ndims, CoreMLInput** out);
int coreml_add_input(CoreMLInput* input, const char* name, 
                     const int64_t* data, const int64_t* shape, int ndims);

// Run inference
int coreml_predict(CoreMLModel* model, CoreMLInput* input, 
                   float* output_data, int64_t output_size, CoreMLError* err);

// Cleanup
void coreml_free_model(CoreMLModel* model);
void coreml_free_input(CoreMLInput* input);
```

**Checklist:**
- [ ] Create coreml_wrapper.h with opaque handle types and function declarations
- [ ] Create coreml_wrapper.m implementing MLModel load, MLDictionaryFeatureProvider, prediction
- [ ] Update build.rs to compile .m file (cc crate supports ObjC)
- [ ] Add Rust bindings in lib.rs: CoreMLModel, CoreMLInput structs with safe wrappers
- [ ] Unit test: load the .mlpackage produced by card 1, run inference, get output
- [ ] Ensure existing ONNX Runtime functionality is not affected
- [ ] All tests pass
\ No newline at end of file
+Replace the planned ObjC wrapper approach with the existing `coreml-rs` crate (https://github.com/swarnimarun/coreml-rs).

`coreml-rs` provides direct Rust bindings to CoreML: load .mlpackage, set ANE compute options, create inputs, run predictions. No need for custom Objective-C wrappers.

**Why coreml-rs:**
- Purpose-built for loading .mlpackage from Rust
- Supports `CoreMLModelOptions` for ANE compute targeting
- Has `.add_input()` / `.predict()` / `.bytesFrom()` API
- No heavy framework dependencies (unlike candle-coreml)
- Active development (72+ commits)

**Alternatives considered:**
- `candle-coreml` — pulls in entire Candle ecosystem, overkill
- `objc2-core-ml` — low-level objc2 bindings, essentially writing the ObjC wrapper ourselves
- Custom ObjC wrapper in onnxruntime-coreml-sys — rejected per ane-embed-crate-plan.md

**Checklist:**
- [ ] Add `coreml-rs` to ane-embedding/Cargo.toml
- [ ] Verify it compiles on macOS arm64
- [ ] Write a minimal test: load the .mlpackage from card 1, run inference, get output
- [ ] Document any API quirks or version pins needed
- [ ] All tests pass
\ No newline at end of file
